permitInfo: Any dumpster placed on a public street or sidewalk in Chicago requires a permit from the Chicago Department of Transportation (CDOT). Apply through the City of Chicago Inspection, Permitting & Licensing portal at ipi.cityofchicago.org. CDOT offers 3-day and 30-day permit durations. Applicants must provide proof of liability insurance of plus a letter of credit. Permit fees vary by location — dumpsters in the Central Business District carry a higher fee. We handle permit applications on your behalf for street placements.
transferStation: Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station, 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910). Accepts C&D debris including wood, concrete, drywall, shingles, brick, and metal. Mon–Fri 6am–5pm, Sat 7am–12pm. Also: LRS Chicago Transfer Station on the Southwest Side accepts C&D, concrete, roofing, and yard waste — call LRS at 773-579-1200 for current hours.
localNote: Chicago's dense urban neighborhoods often have alleys, which make rear placement possible and frequently eliminates the need for a street permit. However, two-flat and greystone gut remodels generate heavy plaster and masonry debris — Chicago's older building stock means your load will often run heavier than the national average, so weight overages are common on interior demo jobs. Budget accordingly.

permitInfo: Naperville requires a Right-of-Way (ROW) permit from the City of Naperville Public Works Department for any dumpster placed in a city-owned right-of-way (street, alley, parkway, or sidewalk). Apply through the City's Permits and Licenses portal at naperville.il.us or call (630) 420-6100. The permit application must include a site plan or aerial showing exact dumpster placement, rental dates, traffic control devices, and any sidewalk impacts. The City requests up to 14 calendar days for ROW permit review. Dumpsters placed entirely on private driveways or construction sites do not require a ROW permit.
transferStation: Midwest Material Management (MMM), 370 S. Brier Hill Rd., Hampshire, IL 60140 (847-426-6354) — a LEED-certified C&D recycling and disposal facility serving DuPage County. For closer drop-off, LRS West Chicago Transfer Station (DuPage/Kane border area) also accepts C&D and construction debris; contact LRS at 630-377-7700. The City of Naperville's Household Hazardous Waste facility is at 156 Fort Hill Drive, open Saturdays and Sundays 9am–2pm (not for C&D debris).
localNote: Naperville is one of the fastest-growing cities in the Midwest, with a large volume of kitchen remodels, basement finishing, and deck tear-offs each spring. DuPage County's strict C&D documentation requirements mean dumpster rental companies must provide certified disposal receipts — reputable haulers do this automatically. Naperville driveways tend to be long and suburban, so most residential placements avoid the street and skip the ROW permit entirely.

permitInfo: Evanston requires a Right-of-Way (ROW) permit from the Evanston Public Works Agency for any dumpster placed in a public right-of-way (street, alley, parkway, or sidewalk). Contact the Public Works Agency at 2100 Ridge Ave., Evanston, IL 60201, phone 847-448-4311 (or dial 3-1-1). The dumpster must be clearly marked with the hauler's name and phone number and covered when not in use. We handle all permit paperwork for street placements.
transferStation: LRS Chicago Transfer Station (Southwest Chicago) accepts C&D and construction debris from North Shore clients — call 773-579-1200. Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station at 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 is also an option for larger loads. For recycling, Evanston's Public Works drop-off is available for some materials at 2020 Asbury Ave.
localNote: Evanston's historic housing stock — Victorian homes, brick two-flats, and large Craftsman bungalows built before 1940 — means remodel dumpsters frequently contain heavy plaster lathe, old-growth lumber, and brick. These materials weigh significantly more than modern drywall and framing, so 10-yard dumpsters are often the right call for a single-room gut. Northwestern University's campus also generates seasonal move-out volume each May and August.

permitInfo: Oak Park requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Oak Park Public Works Department for any dumpster placed on a public street or in the parkway. Apply by contacting Public Works at 123 Madison St., Oak Park, IL 60302, phone (708) 358-5740. Dumpsters placed entirely on private property (driveways, parking areas) do not require a permit. The dumpster must be marked with reflective tape and the hauler's contact information.
transferStation: Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station, 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) is the primary C&D facility serving Oak Park. Also, LRS operates transfer facilities on Chicago's West Side for construction debris — call 773-579-1200 for routing.
localNote: Oak Park is home to the largest concentration of Frank Lloyd Wright-designed structures in the world, along with hundreds of Prairie-style and Victorian homes built between 1890 and 1930. Renovation work in Oak Park often involves original plaster walls, old-growth hardwood flooring, and heavy masonry — all of which add substantial weight to a dumpster load. A 10-yard box is frequently the right size for a single-room gut in these older homes.

permitInfo: Schaumburg requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Village of Schaumburg Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in any public right-of-way, including streets and parkways. Contact Public Works at 101 Schaumburg Court, Schaumburg, IL 60193, phone (847) 923-3900. Dumpsters on private driveways or commercial lots do not require a permit. Reflective markers are required on all street-placed containers.
transferStation: Waste Management Northwest Suburban Transfer Station, 1300 E. Irving Park Rd., Streamwood, IL 60107 (847-289-7700) serves Schaumburg-area C&D disposal. The facility accepts wood, drywall, roofing shingles, and mixed construction debris.
localNote: Schaumburg is a major commercial hub in the northwest suburbs, home to Woodfield Mall and a large concentration of corporate campuses and office parks. This means dumpster rental volume skews toward commercial buildouts and tenant improvement projects in addition to residential work. Residential neighborhoods are mostly post-1970 construction with standard drywall and framing — loads run lighter than Chicago's older housing stock.

permitInfo: Arlington Heights requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Village of Arlington Heights Community Development Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact the department at 33 S. Arlington Heights Rd., Arlington Heights, IL 60005, phone (847) 368-5200. Dumpsters on private property do not require a permit. Street-placed dumpsters must be equipped with reflective markers.
transferStation: Waste Management Northwest Suburban Transfer Station, 1300 E. Irving Park Rd., Streamwood, IL 60107 (847-289-7700) is the primary C&D disposal facility for Arlington Heights. Accepts mixed C&D, roofing shingles, drywall, and lumber.
localNote: Arlington Heights is one of the northwest suburbs' most established communities, with housing stock ranging from post-war Cape Cods and ranch homes to newer construction subdivisions. The village sees steady renovation activity each spring, particularly roof replacements and kitchen remodels. Most properties have driveways long enough to place a dumpster off-street, which avoids the ROW permit process entirely.

permitInfo: Skokie requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Village of Skokie Public Works Department for any dumpster placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 5127 Oakton St., Skokie, IL 60077, phone (847) 933-8427. Dumpsters placed on private driveways or property do not require a permit. Containers must be marked with reflective tape and the hauler's phone number.
transferStation: Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station, 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) serves North Shore suburbs including Skokie. LRS also operates a transfer facility on Chicago's North Side — call 773-579-1200 for the nearest drop location.
localNote: Skokie has a dense, established residential character with many homes built in the 1950s and 1960s — split-levels, brick ranches, and small colonials are common. These homes are increasingly being renovated or sold as estate cleanouts, driving consistent dumpster rental demand. Lots tend to be small and driveways short, so street placement is common in many neighborhoods. The village's flat terrain makes dumpster delivery straightforward.

permitInfo: Cicero requires a permit from the Town of Cicero Department of Public Works for any dumpster placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 4937 W. 25th St., Cicero, IL 60804, phone (708) 656-3600. Dumpsters on private property do not require a permit. Cicero uses a town government structure rather than a village or city, so permitting runs through the Town Administrator's office.
transferStation: Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station, 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) is convenient for Cicero C&D debris. Also, LRS operates transfer facilities on Chicago's Southwest Side — call 773-579-1200 for current routing.
localNote: Cicero is one of the most densely populated suburbs of Chicago and has housing stock that closely mirrors Chicago's own — brick bungalows, Chicago-style two-flats, and courtyard apartments built from the 1920s through the 1950s. Renovation loads often include heavy plaster, old brick, and clay tile, which can push a 10-yard dumpster to its weight limit quickly. Alleys are common in Cicero, allowing rear-of-property placement without a street permit.

permitInfo: Aurora requires a Right-of-Way permit from the City of Aurora Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in any public right-of-way. Apply by contacting Aurora Public Works at 400 W. Illinois Ave., Aurora, IL 60506, phone (630) 256-3700. Dumpsters on private property do not require a permit. Aurora spans both Kane and DuPage counties — permit requirements are the same city-wide regardless of county.
transferStation: Midwest Material Management (MMM), 370 S. Brier Hill Rd., Hampshire, IL 60140 (847-426-6354) serves Kane County C&D disposal. Republic Services also operates a transfer station in the Aurora area — call (630) 896-8900 for current hours and accepted materials.
localNote: Aurora is the second-largest city in Illinois and spans both Kane and DuPage counties, giving it a wide range of housing types — from historic downtown Victorian homes and brick bungalows near the Fox River to modern suburban subdivisions in the far reaches. The older East Aurora neighborhoods generate heavy demo debris similar to Chicago, while West Aurora's newer construction runs lighter. Confirm your address's county when booking, as disposal routing may differ.

permitInfo: Joliet requires a Right-of-Way permit from the City of Joliet Division of Engineering for dumpsters placed in any public right-of-way. Contact Engineering at 150 W. Jefferson St., Joliet, IL 60432, phone (815) 724-4200. Dumpsters on private property or construction sites within fenced areas do not require a permit. Will County has separate regulations for unincorporated areas — confirm your address is within Joliet city limits.
transferStation: Will County Solid Waste Management Facility, 2050 W. Laraway Rd., Joliet, IL 60431 (815-727-8476) accepts C&D debris including concrete, drywall, roofing materials, and wood. Waste Management also operates a transfer station in the Joliet area — call (815) 727-7760 for routing and hours.
localNote: Joliet is Will County's largest city and has experienced significant growth, with a mix of older historic neighborhoods near downtown and large post-2000 subdivisions on the outskirts. The older areas near the Des Plaines River have brick and masonry construction typical of late 1800s–early 1900s housing. Joliet also sees periodic storm-related dumpster demand after severe weather passes through the I-55 corridor. Drive times from Chicago are longer — factor in fuel surcharges from some haulers.

permitInfo: Elgin requires a Right-of-Way permit from the City of Elgin Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 150 Dexter Court, Elgin, IL 60120, phone (847) 931-6001. Dumpsters placed entirely on private property do not require a permit. Elgin spans both Kane and Cook counties — ROW permit requirements are the same citywide.
transferStation: Midwest Material Management (MMM), 370 S. Brier Hill Rd., Hampshire, IL 60140 (847-426-6354) is the primary C&D disposal facility for the Elgin area. Accepts construction and demolition debris including concrete, drywall, lumber, and roofing shingles.
localNote: Elgin has a historic downtown core along the Fox River with housing stock dating to the mid-1800s, including a National Register Historic District. Renovations in these older neighborhoods often involve heavy plaster, masonry, and original hardwood materials. The city also has extensive post-1980 suburban development to the south and west, where lighter modern construction is standard. Elgin's distance from Chicago (40+ miles) means some haulers add a small fuel surcharge.

permitInfo: Waukegan requires a Right-of-Way permit from the City of Waukegan Department of Public Works for dumpsters placed in any public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 2505 Belvidere Rd., Waukegan, IL 60085, phone (847) 360-9100. Dumpsters on private property do not require a permit. Lake County has environmental regulations around disposal near the shoreline — confirm materials are properly sorted if working near Lake Michigan.
transferStation: Lake County Solid Waste Agency Transfer Station, 1814 Lewis Ave., Zion, IL 60099 (847-623-4747) accepts C&D debris for Lake County residents and contractors. Also, Waste Management operates a transfer facility in the Waukegan area — call (847) 623-4800 for current hours.
localNote: Waukegan is Lake County's largest city and seat, with older housing stock concentrated in the downtown area near the lakefront. Many homes in Waukegan date to the early 20th century, generating heavier demo loads with plaster and masonry. The city is one of the more affordable Lake County communities, which drives steady renovation and flip activity. Lake County's proximity to Lake Michigan means occasional shoreline-related debris rules — check with your hauler if working near the water.

permitInfo: Berwyn requires a permit from the City of Berwyn Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 6700 W. 26th St., Berwyn, IL 60402, phone (708) 788-2660. Dumpsters on private driveways or property do not require a permit. The city requires haulers to be licensed with the city before operating within Berwyn limits.
transferStation: Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station, 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) serves Berwyn's C&D disposal needs. The facility accepts wood, drywall, concrete, masonry, and roofing materials.
localNote: Berwyn is one of the most densely developed suburbs immediately west of Chicago and contains an exceptionally well-preserved stock of Craftsman bungalows, brick two-flats, and foursquares built between 1910 and 1940. These are the same construction type as Chicago's famous bungalow belt — heavy plaster walls, clay tile, old-growth lumber, and masonry foundations. Weight overages are common on interior demo jobs. Most blocks have alleys, so rear placement is often possible and avoids street permit requirements.

permitInfo: Des Plaines requires a Right-of-Way permit from the City of Des Plaines Public Works and Engineering Department for dumpsters placed in any public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 1420 Miner St., Des Plaines, IL 60016, phone (847) 391-5390. Dumpsters on private property do not require a permit. O'Hare Airport flight paths run over parts of Des Plaines — this has no bearing on permits but is a local landmark often referenced in the area.
transferStation: Waste Management Northwest Suburban Transfer Station, 1300 E. Irving Park Rd., Streamwood, IL 60107 (847-289-7700) serves Des Plaines-area C&D disposal. Also, LRS operates a transfer facility in the northwest suburban corridor — call 847-579-1200 for current routing.
localNote: Des Plaines is a mature northwest suburban community with a strong mix of post-war ranch homes and 1960s–1970s split-levels. The city sits along the Des Plaines River and has experienced periodic flooding, which drives demand for basement cleanouts and water-damage debris removal. Des Plaines' proximity to O'Hare Airport also supports a steady commercial market for tenant improvement and facility maintenance dumpsters.

permitInfo: Orland Park requires a permit from the Village of Orland Park Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 14700 Ravinia Ave., Orland Park, IL 60462, phone (708) 403-6165. Dumpsters on private driveways or construction sites do not require a permit. Many Orland Park residential properties have wide driveways, making off-street placement the norm.
transferStation: Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station, 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) is the primary C&D disposal facility serving the south and southwest suburbs including Orland Park. Accepts wood, drywall, roofing, and mixed C&D.
localNote: Orland Park is an affluent south suburban community known for large lot sizes and well-maintained ranch and two-story colonial homes, many built in the 1980s and 1990s. Renovation projects here tend to be full kitchen and bathroom remodels rather than structural gut-outs. Driveways are typically wide and long enough for dumpster placement without street permits. Spring is the busiest season for deck tear-offs and landscaping debris.

permitInfo: Bolingbrook requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Village of Bolingbrook Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 375 W. Briarcliff Rd., Bolingbrook, IL 60440, phone (630) 226-8450. Dumpsters placed entirely on private property do not require a permit. Bolingbrook spans Will and DuPage counties — permit requirements are uniform across the village.
transferStation: Will County Solid Waste Management Facility, 2050 W. Laraway Rd., Joliet, IL 60431 (815-727-8476) serves the Will County portion of Bolingbrook. For DuPage County addresses within Bolingbrook, LRS West Chicago Transfer Station (630-377-7700) is an alternative.
localNote: Bolingbrook is a planned suburb that grew rapidly from the 1960s through the 1990s, resulting in large neighborhoods of ranch homes, split-levels, and colonials on spacious lots. Most homes have long driveways suitable for dumpster placement without a street permit. The village is bisected by I-55, making logistics straightforward for haulers. Bolingbrook spans two counties (Will and DuPage), so confirm which county your address falls in when scheduling.

permitInfo: Glenview requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Village of Glenview Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in any public right-of-way including streets and parkways. Contact Public Works at 1225 Waukegan Rd., Glenview, IL 60025, phone (847) 724-1700. Dumpsters on private driveways do not require a permit. The Former Glenview Naval Air Station redevelopment area (The Glen) may have additional site-specific permitting requirements.
transferStation: LRS North Shore Transfer Station serves the Glenview area for C&D disposal — call LRS at 847-579-1200 for current location and hours. Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station at 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) is also accessible for larger loads.
localNote: Glenview is one of the wealthier North Shore suburbs, with large colonial and ranch homes on expansive lots. Renovation projects here tend to be high-end full remodels — whole kitchens, master suites, finished basements — generating multiple dumpster loads. Lot sizes are generous, so off-street placement is almost always possible. The redeveloped Former Naval Air Station area (The Glen) has newer construction and active commercial development.

permitInfo: Northbrook requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Village of Northbrook Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 1225 Cedar Lane, Northbrook, IL 60062, phone (847) 664-4100. Dumpsters placed entirely on private driveways or construction sites within property lines do not require a permit. Northbrook's tree-lined streets mean haulers must take care not to damage parkway trees during delivery.
transferStation: LRS North Shore Transfer Station serves Northbrook for C&D debris — call LRS at 847-579-1200 for routing. Tri-State Disposal at 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) handles larger loads from the northern suburbs.
localNote: Northbrook is an affluent North Shore suburb with large custom homes on mature, tree-lined lots. Many properties date from the 1950s–1970s and are undergoing full renovations or teardowns. The tree-lined streets require careful delivery — drivers need to navigate around large parkway oaks and maples when placing containers. Budget for at least a 20-yard dumpster on any full kitchen or bathroom remodel, as these high-end projects generate substantial material volume.

permitInfo: Oak Lawn requires a permit from the Village of Oak Lawn Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 9446 S. Raymond Ave., Oak Lawn, IL 60453, phone (708) 499-7748. Dumpsters on private driveways do not require a permit. The village requires haulers to be licensed to operate within Oak Lawn.
transferStation: Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station, 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) is the primary C&D facility serving Oak Lawn and the south suburbs. Accepts wood, drywall, roofing, concrete, and mixed C&D debris.
localNote: Oak Lawn is a southwest Cook County suburb with a mix of brick bungalows and ranch homes, largely built between the 1940s and 1960s. The housing stock is denser and older than many southern suburbs, generating moderate demo loads. Many homes are on relatively small lots with short driveways, so street placement is common. The village is near Advocate Christ Medical Center, and the surrounding area sees consistent healthcare-adjacent commercial renovation activity.

permitInfo: Tinley Park requires a permit from the Village of Tinley Park Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 17355 S. 68th Court, Tinley Park, IL 60477, phone (708) 444-5500. Dumpsters placed on private driveways or within a construction site on private property do not require a permit. The village spans Cook and Will counties, but permitting requirements are uniform throughout.
transferStation: Tri-State Disposal Transfer Station, 13903 S. Ashland Ave., Riverdale, IL 60827 (708-388-9910) serves the south and southwest suburbs including Tinley Park for C&D debris disposal.
localNote: Tinley Park is a well-established south suburb that grew substantially in the 1980s and 1990s, resulting in large subdivisions of colonials and ranch homes. The housing stock is younger than many closer-in suburbs, so demo loads tend to run lighter with standard drywall and framing rather than plaster. The village is known as an entertainment destination (Hollywood Casino Amphitheater is here) and has a strong commercial corridor along Oak Park Ave. Most residential driveways are long enough to place a dumpster without needing a street permit.

permitInfo: Downers Grove requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Village of Downers Grove Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in the public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 5101 Walnut Ave., Downers Grove, IL 60515, phone (630) 434-5460. Dumpsters placed entirely on private property do not require a permit. DuPage County's strict waste disposal tracking requirements mean licensed haulers must provide certified weight tickets and disposal receipts.
transferStation: Midwest Material Management (MMM), 370 S. Brier Hill Rd., Hampshire, IL 60140 (847-426-6354) accepts DuPage County C&D debris. LRS West Chicago Transfer Station also serves Downers Grove — call LRS at 630-377-7700 for current hours and accepted materials.
localNote: Downers Grove is one of DuPage County's oldest and most charming suburbs, with a historic downtown and a wide range of housing from late-Victorian homes near Main Street to post-war ranches and 1980s colonials in newer subdivisions. The older neighborhoods near the Metra station have homes with original plaster walls and heavier construction typical of early 20th century building. DuPage County requires certified disposal documentation — make sure your hauler provides compliant waste receipts.

permitInfo: Palatine requires a Right-of-Way permit from the Village of Palatine Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in any public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 148 W. Wilson St., Palatine, IL 60067, phone (847) 359-9050. Dumpsters on private property or driveways do not require a permit. The village requires proof of hauler licensing and liability insurance for permitted placements.
transferStation: Waste Management Northwest Suburban Transfer Station, 1300 E. Irving Park Rd., Streamwood, IL 60107 (847-289-7700) serves Palatine-area C&D disposal. The facility accepts wood, drywall, shingles, and mixed construction debris.
localNote: Palatine is a large northwest suburban community with a mix of housing types — townhome complexes, single-family subdivisions from the 1970s–1990s, and older homes near the downtown Metra station. The downtown area around the Palatine Metra station has seen revitalization investment and restaurant/retail buildouts. Most residential properties have adequate driveway space for off-street dumpster placement, avoiding the need for a ROW permit.

permitInfo: Wheaton requires a Right-of-Way permit from the City of Wheaton Public Works Department for dumpsters placed in any public right-of-way. Contact Public Works at 303 W. Wesley St., Wheaton, IL 60187, phone (630) 260-2000. Dumpsters placed entirely on private property do not require a permit. As DuPage County's seat, Wheaton enforces county-level waste disposal documentation requirements — licensed haulers must provide certified disposal receipts.
transferStation: Midwest Material Management (MMM), 370 S. Brier Hill Rd., Hampshire, IL 60140 (847-426-6354) accepts DuPage County C&D debris including lumber, drywall, concrete, and roofing shingles. LRS West Chicago Transfer Station is also accessible for Wheaton — call LRS at 630-377-7700.
localNote: Wheaton is the DuPage County seat and one of the western suburbs' most established communities, home to Wheaton College and a charming historic downtown. Housing ranges from Victorian-era homes near downtown to large post-war colonials and newer subdivisions. The older homes closer to the Metra station have heavier construction with plaster walls and original hardwood flooring — budget for potential weight overages on interior demolition jobs. DuPage County's disposal documentation rules mean your hauler must provide certified waste receipts.
